sections.paragraph:
root unused in Kal, i.q. חָשַׂף to strip off bark, to peel, to scale. Arab. حسف to peel dates, and transp. ساحف to scrape or rub off; hence Ch. חֲסַף; Arab. خَزَفُ and حرشف a scale, a sherd; Syr. ܚܨܶܦܐܳ id. There are of the same origin in the Western languages, σκάπτω, scabo, squama; German fchaben, fchuppen, Schuppe, Scherbe, Schiefer, fchaufeln [Eng. scab, scale, sherd]; in all of which the sibilant comes first, as in Hebr. and Arab. סָחַף, ساحف.

Quadril. חַסְפַּס part. pass. מְחֻסְפָּס Exo 16:14, something peeled off, scaled off, i.e. like a scale. Hence-
